test_cases.test: basis = orthonormal_basis([[1, 0], [1, 1]]); print([b.round(4) for b in basis])
basis = orthonormal_basis([[2, 0], [4, 0]], tol=1e-10); print([b.round(4) for b in basis])

example.reasoning: Start with [1, 0], normalize to [1, 0]. For [1, 1], subtract its projection onto [1, 0] (which is [1, 0]), leaving [0, 1]. Check if norm > 1e-10 (it is 1), then normalize to [0, 1]. The result is an orthonormal basis.

starter_code:
import numpy as np

def orthonormal_basis(vectors: list[list[float]], tol: float = 1e-10) -> list[np.ndarray]:
    # Your code here

title: Compute Orthonormal Basis for 2D Vectors

pytorch_test_cases.test:
basis = orthonormal_basis([[1, 0], [1, 1]])
print([b.numpy().round(4).tolist() for b in basis])

basis = orthonormal_basis([[2, 0], [4, 0]], tol=1e-10)
print([b.numpy().round(4).tolist() for b in basis])

basis = orthonormal_basis([[1, 1], [1, -1]], tol=1e-5)
print([[round(x, 4) for x in b.tolist()] for b in basis])

basis = orthonormal_basis([[0, 0]], tol=1e-10)
print([b.numpy().round(4).tolist() for b in basis])

tinygrad_test_cases.test:
import numpy as np
basis = orthonormal_basis([[1, 0], [1, 1]])
print([[round(float(x), 4) for x in b.numpy()] for b in basis])

import numpy as np
basis = orthonormal_basis([[2, 0], [4, 0]], tol=1e-10)
print([[round(float(x), 4) for x in b.numpy()] for b in basis])

import numpy as np
basis = orthonormal_basis([[1, 1], [1, -1]], tol=1e-5)
print([[round(float(x), 4) for x in b.numpy()] for b in basis])

basis = orthonormal_basis([[0, 0]], tol=1e-10)
